commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review should gate the security-boundary repair, compatibility policy choice, and required contributor proof before merge.; Needs attention: The patch weakens the workspace avatar file-read boundary by replacing the existing opened-fd data-URI read with a path read after validation.; Review finding: [P1] Keep avatar data-URI reads on a root-safe file descriptor — src/agents/identity-avatar.ts:151 (sha=f27ab043c88814a935e58f37f33568c3eb229449)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves workspace-relative agent avatar data-URI projection into `src/agents/identity-avatar.ts`, reuses it from `agent.identity.get`, `agents.list`, and Control UI bootstrap, and updates Personal-card avatar tests.

PR surface: Source -12, Tests +126. Total +114 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can return `/avatar/<agentId>` from `agent.identity.get` for workspace-relative avatars while the Control UI avatar route requires read authorization; no live browser session was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should gate the security-boundary repair, compatibility policy choice, and required contributor proof before merge.

target.title: fix: render workspace avatars in identity card
target.body:
﻿Closes #97602

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users configuring a workspace-relative agent avatar would see a broken avatar in the Control UI Personal card because identity/bootstrap payloads could hand the browser an auth-gated `/avatar/<agentId>` URL that a plain image element cannot load with Bearer auth.

## Why This Change Was Made

Avatar URL projection now lives with the existing agent avatar owner code in `src/agents/identity-avatar.ts` instead of duplicating workspace file handling in gateway session projection. `agent.identity.get`, `listAgentsForGateway`, and the Control UI bootstrap config all reuse that owner-level resolver for configured workspace-relative avatars.

The Control UI Personal card now prefers renderable identity data URLs when the backend has already resolved one, while still avoiding stale `/avatar/<agentId>` route previews when avatar resolution rejected the source.

AI-assisted with Codex.

## User Impact

Control UI can render valid workspace-relative Personal card avatars consistently across bootstrap, identity refresh, agent list, and chat-adjacent identity surfaces, without making unauthenticated `/avatar/<agentId>` image requests. Unsupported local avatar files remain blocked and reported through the existing avatar status/reason fields.

## Evidence

- Red/green backend regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -- -t "returns workspace-relative avatars as data URLs"`
- Red/green backend security regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -- -t "does not inline unsupported workspace avatar files"`
- Red/green UI route regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts -- -t "keeps identity data URLs renderable"`
- Red/green UI rejected-source regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts -- -t "does not render stale route avatars"`
- Focused identity coverage: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -- -t "agent.identity.get"`
- Avatar projection coverage: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts -- -t "avatar"`
- Bootstrap config coverage: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts -- -t "bootstrap config"`
- UI render coverage: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/config-quick.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Notes:
- Full `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` on this Windows checkout still has unrelated pre-existing failures around SQLite temp cleanup `EBUSY` and `/tmp` path normalization, so focused avatar coverage was used for this PR.
- Local autoreview was attempted after the final refactor, but the autoreview wrapper failed before engine review with `WinError 267` while resolving `git` from this Windows checkout path. Earlier post-fix autoreview also hit the Codex account usage limit, so there is no successful fresh autoreview result to claim.
